feed.title: SAP Community - SAP S/4HANA
feed.subtitle: SAP S/4HANA Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: Automating S/4HANA Cloud UI Deployment via CI/CD Pipeline
entry.content:
I'm currently developing an OnStack Extension on the S/4HANA Public Cloud. This includes a UI generated using SAP Fiori Elements. The development takes place in VSCode. For deployment to the S/4HANA Cloud, the @SAP/ux-ui5-tooling npm package is used, specifically the command fiori deploy --config ui5-deploy.yaml.

The current ui5-deploy.yaml looks as follows:

specVersion: "3.1"
metadata:
  name: xxx
type: application
builder:
  customTasks:
    - name: ui5-tooling-transpile-task
      afterTask: replaceVersion
      configuration:
        debug: true
        transformModulesToUI5:
          overridesToOverride: true
    - name: deploy-to-abap
      afterTask: generateCachebusterInfo
      configuration:
        target:
          url: xxx
          authenticationType: reentranceTicket
        app:
          name: xxx
          description: xxx
          package: xxx
          transport: xxx
        exclude:
          - /test/
          - /*.ts
  resources:
    excludes:
      - /test/**
      - /localService/**

This means that the deployment is currently executed in the user context using a reentrance ticket. Now, I want to automate the deployment via a CI/CD pipeline (either Azure DevOps or SAP CI/CD Service).

The challenge is that a reentrance ticket cannot be generated in an automated context. My idea was to use a technical user, but I couldn’t find a suitable Communication Scenario that would support this kind of deployment.

What would be the best way to accomplish this?

entry.title: Company Code Creation - OX02
entry.content: What is the main difference between PO Box, Postal Code and Company Postal Code and why cannot we maintain all three together ?

entry.title: Which values can be used for valuating goods receipt from a production order?
entry.content:
Hello!

Which values can be used for valuating goods receipt from a production order?
There is no material cost estimate and no sales order costing available. The value for the goods receipt is taken from the planned cost estimate of the production order.
Is there a configuration option—similar to the valuation variant for material withdrawals—where the system checks available values in a defined sequence? What does it depends on? 

 

Thank you and kind regards

Olga

entry.title: Why message displayed shows no text, only the variable 1? (BAdI BD_MMPUR_FINAL_CHECK_PO)
entry.content:
I implemented the BAdI BD_MMPUR_FINAL_CHECK_PO to do custom checks in ME22N and F0842A (purchase order).

I fill the return parameter MESSAGES with message 001 in message class ZPO ("there is an error concerning item &1") with variable 1 "00020" but the displayed error message is "00020" alone.

I expect "there is an error concerning item 00020"

Note that the message exists in English language, and ABAP code running in English.

MESSAGE e001(zpo) WITH '00020' INTO DATA(lv_message).
messages = VALUE #( ( messageid        = sy-msgid
                      messagetype      = sy-msgty
                      messagenumber    = sy-msgno
                      messagevariable1 = sy-msgv1
                      messagevariable2 = sy-msgv2
                      messagevariable3 = sy-msgv3
                      messagevariable4 = sy-msgv4 ) ).

What's the problem with the code?

Thanks.

Sandra

System: S/4HANA 2023 FPS 01 / On premises.

entry.title: [S/4HANA] What is the use of Technical User(No Address User) in SU01? Why needed?
entry.content:
Nice to meet you.
I'm a newbie who's just been put into SAP ERP System management.

When I was using SU01 regarding user management, I found the difference between ECC and S/4HANA, so I asked you.

In S/4HANA, there is a Technical User Create button apart from the Normal User Create button when creating a user in SU01.

Can you tell me why you need that kind of user and what that user is for?

 

Below is what I looked for about the Technical User.

As far as I know, Technical User does not have the Address tab, so the description of that user should be written in the Documentation tab.
Other than that, we found no other difference, and what we found in SU01's assistant document is no different.
  ※ There was only one paragraph below related information that can be found in SU01.
      Differences Between Users and Maintaining Their Address Data
      Technical User: A technical user does not have any address data.
                               Use the corresponding field on the "Documentation" tab for the description.

      If there's any other difference I've missed, please share!

(For example, is there a difference like User Type: System users, where the password-related parameters are not applied?)

 

When I checked the Help.sap documents, there was only a description of the Technical User in other SAP solutions.
In the case of Technical User on S/4HANA, there is only a description of how to create a technical user (Create Technical User) and no documentation was found to describe what an actual technical user was.

At first, I thought it was a system or external communication user, but I think this is a more appropriate description for users who correspond to User Type: System or Communications Data.

 

I simply didn't understand why it was necessary to create a separate feature for a user who didn't have Address Data.
For the other fields, it appears to be a function that exists not to enter "Last Name" as a non-essential input element.

Normally, I was typing a description of the user in that field, but is there a significant role I don't know about in that field, so is there a need to empty that field?

Could you please share with me the true use of the Technical User?

entry.title: Bapi is is not working SD_SALESDOCUMENT_CREATE
entry.content:
Hi Team 

We are using BAPI - SD_SALESDOCUMENT_CREATE for sales order return , but it is  not determinig  condtional value custom condition type  .

when we are creating return order manually by  VA01 it is workign fine .

entry.title: Monitor S.O. vs outbound delivery number
entry.content:
Hello all,

"We are going live in S4H/C on May 1, and during the hyper care period, I am looking for an app or another way to create an overview of the sales order numbers and outbound delivery numbers at a glance. Ideally, I would prefer to use the confirmed delivery date as a filter. This way, we can see which orders are planned for specific days based on the outbound delivery number."

Please share your'e knowledge with me

entry.title: Custom Fields in Contract Cash Flow (Fiori App F5491)
entry.content:
Dear Community,
I have created some additional fields for RE-FX Contracts in the “Custom Fields and Logic” app (F1481) and activated them for all possible objects.
The fields are available in the “Contract Management” app (F5273), for example, and I can also make them visible and use them in the SAP GUI by customizing the sections/screens and Badis etc..
However, the fields are not visible in the APP “Contract Cash Flow” (F5491), although they are visible in the GUI transaction REISCDCF.
Do any of you know the reason for this? Is there anything I can do to get the additional fields into the report?

Thanks in advance for any advice!

Regards
Rado

entry.title: Our customer wants to export entries in entry view, ACDOCA has document splitting,
entry.content:
Hi Team,

Our new Chinese company code has a legal requirement to store all the financial data posted in SAP, maintained in Kingdee system (Chinese government system). When we try to build a report, we face issues with entries in ACDOCA (New universal ledger table in S4HANA). ACDOCA storing entries in the document splitting view.

Businesses want only entries in the entry view (Same as BSEG Table), Is there any way we can actually get entry view line items from ACDOCA table without document splitting line items (Like zero balance line item, Split line item). or is there any other table which we can use as BSEG is no longer used in the future in S4HANA.

We also noticed tax line items are split as per profit centre in ACDOCA and amounts appearing differently in the table which is confusing.

Please let us know how do we solve this issue, Please find the attached document for more details

entry.title: different e-mail adresses for sending purchase order and consignment settlement
entry.content:
We would like to send the consignment settlement message (output type KONS) to our supplier via e-mail to a different e-mail adress than the purchase order. 

In SAP standard I found no possibility to manage that. As described in SAP Help we use the standard programm RM08NAST with form ENTRY_KONS_PDF. 

The program is always taking the standard e-mail adress from business partner and I have no possibility to change it.  Is there any idea how we can send the message to another recipient?

entry.title: SAP CCMS intergrate dingtalk
entry.content:
Hello, everyone How to send the SAP CCMS alerts to dingtalk，How to realize this requirement, thanks.

Best Regards,

hayden

entry.title: Questionaire for Change log of Profit Segment in Sales Order VA02
entry.content:
Dear SAP Supports,

We are facing a problem as follows:

 - We create a normal sales order with VA01.
 - User changes the Sales Office of the Line Item in tab Account Assignment

entry.title: Install on-premise partner add-ons for S/4HANA 2023 private cloud
entry.content:
Hi Experts, 

We had released add-ons(partner) for our customers and they are on-premise. 

Is there any difference in installing the partner add-ons on S/4HANA private cloud or its same as like for on-premise? 

What is the procedure to release private cloud compatible add-ons and customers to install them on private cloud?

Thanks,

Prabhakaran

entry.title: partition (1) contains *** records. A table partition cannot contain more than 2,147,483,64
entry.content:
The Table S*** has 2 partitions as we are reaching 2Billion mark, but we are observing that the Partition-1 of the the table is still increasing by few entries everyday. it is very close to 2Billion records and we would need suggestion on the house keeping for this table to keep the entries under the limit.Please find the screenshot of Historical Growth based on Daily Data: 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Create item level with the logic
entry.content:
I have SellingPrice and BuyingPrice in both Header level and Item level. And has a "Copy" button in Header level. 

What I want to do is when user click the Copy button, the value of SellingPrice and BuyingPrice in header level will be assigned to item level and one record is created. 

Everytime user click the Copy button, item level should be created with the value of Header level data.

Below is the logic I have written in Copy action. But it gets error of "Last runtime error at: 04/28/2025, 04:51:37, error text: The key of business object YY1_PRICINGINFORMATION is wrongly specified." 

Although the YY1_PRICINGINFORMATION is correct, it gives the same error. The key has value "0000000001"

* Step 1: Get Root Entity Key
*DATA(lv_key_cleaned) = PRICINGINFORMATION-PriceID. 
DATA(lv_key) = |{ PRICINGINFORMATION-PriceID }|.

* Step 2: Get the root node object
DATA(lo_root) = write->get_root(
business_object_id = 'YY1_PRICINGINFORMATION' 
key = 'lv_key'
).

* Step 3: Prepare data for child node (ITEM)
DATA(ls_item) = VALUE YY1_HISTORY_PRICINGINFORMATION( 
BuyingPrice = PRICINGINFORMATION-Price
SellingPrice = PRICINGINFORMATION-SellingPrice
).

* Step 4: Create child under root
lo_root->create_child(
EXPORTING
node_id = 'History' 
data = ls_item
).

* Step 5: Return success message
message = VALUE #( severity = 'S' text = 'Item created from Action.' ).

 

Could you pls guide us how to change my logic to fulfil requirement? If that way is not okay, could you pls guide me the possible way? 

Thanks in advance. 

Best Regards,

Pyae

entry.title: TM New Attribute to addition selection profile and use it in Planning Cockpit
entry.content:
This blog post explains how to add a custom attribute to the Additional Selection Profile for Freight Units (FU) and Freight Orders (FO), and how to use this custom field in the planning cockpit.


The requirement was to filter the Transportation Cockpit based on a custom field. Since it was not possible to add the custom field directly, an enhancement was implemented to make the custom field available in the Additional Selection Profile. Once added, this custom field can be used to filter results within the Transportation Cockpit.

Step 1: 

Add the custom field ZZVEHICLE_TYPE to the structure /SCMTMS/D_TORROT, and ensure that the save strategy is implemented to populate ZZVEHICLE_TYPE with the required value.

Step 2:

Enhance the structure /SCMTMS/S_TOR_QUERY, which is used in /SCMTMS/S_TOR_Q_FU_STG_PLN, and add the custom field ZZVEHICLE_TYPE

Adding the field ZVEHICLE_TYPE to the above structure will make it available as an attribute in the Additional Selection Profile.

Step 3:

Additionally, implement the method GET_SHLP_FIELD_WHITELIST of class /SCMTMS/CL_PROF_HELPER to allow the custom field to be visible in the Additional Selection Attributes.

Step 4:

Now, the field ZZVEHICLE_TYPE is visible and has been added to the Additional Selection Attributes.

Step 5:

Enhance the method GET_QUERY_ENHANCE_TABLE of class /SCMTMS/CL_TOR_Q_SUPERCLASS to specify the TM database table that should be referenced for the custom Z field added in the selection profile and used for filtering.

After completing the above developments and implementations, the Transportation Cockpit will be able to filter based on the new additional fields in the selection profile.

 

 

I hope this blog post will be helpful to SAP developers.


Regards,
Prince Agarwal

entry.title: S/4 HANA migration : Factors leading to success from a customer perspective
entry.content:
“How We Nailed Our S/4HANA Migration — 4 Hours Ahead of Schedule during Go-live weekend”

As SAP gradually phases out support for ECC by the end of 2027, a vast number of companies are making the move to S/4HANA. In line with this trend, my organization in the utilities sector initiated our migration project in 2023. I’m happy to share that we recently completed a successful go-live — four hours ahead of schedule!

The purpose of this article is to share the key factors that contributed to our success, with the hope that they will support others on the same journey. I am a SAP Certified Associate: SAP Activate Project Manager with experience across various SAP projects. Our migration was predominantly Brownfield, involving a transition from an on-premises setup to a private cloud environment.

Below are the key criteria that made our migration successful:

1. Preparation

SAP provides a comprehensive Readiness Check package free of charge to all migration customers. One of the essential components is the Simplification Item List, which is executed in your SAP ERP system in preparation for the conversion to S/4HANA.[1] This analysis generates a list of:

  • New functionalities available in S/4HANA
  • Deprecated functionalities with recommended alternatives

This list helps the project team assess the impact on each SAP module, enabling more accurate resource allocation and project planning.

Another important preparatory step is addressing knowledge gaps within the team. A Learning Needs Analysis [2] can identify which team members require upskilling to ensure a smooth migration.

2. Design

During the Explore phase of SAP’s Activate methodology, [3] fit-to-standard workshops are conducted. These sessions compare the current (as-is) processes with future (to-be) scenarios and identify any gaps — referred to as Delta Requirements. [4] The project team must then determine whether these gaps can be addressed through standard SAP functionality or require enhancements.

Phases of SAP Activate[5]

Crucially, these workshops are guided by the Simplification Item List, which may include items needing key business decisions. For instance, when a functionality change offers multiple solution paths, the input of business stakeholders — key users and process owners — is essential. Their participation in these workshops ensures the resulting functional design meets business needs.

3. Test Strategy

A solid test strategy, developed during the Explore phase, is essential. Various types of testing occur during the SAP implementation:

  • Unit Testing: Conducted by the implementation partner to verify individual transactions.
  • System Integration Testing: The customer’s responsibility; ensures module dependencies and integrations (SAP and non-SAP) work as expected and data integrity is preserved.
  • User Acceptance Testing (UAT): Validates whether the system supports day-to-day business operations.[6]

Additionally, consider performance and penetration testing, especially for mission-critical systems.

4. Data Validation Strategy

Ensuring the accuracy and completeness of migrated data is crucial — not only for business continuity but also for IT audit compliance. Collaborate with your IT auditor early to identify critical data points, and validate these through comparison of pre- and post-migration datasets in each cycle (SBX, DEV, QAS, PROD). A thorough validation process enables the IT auditor to approve the decommissioning of ECC systems confidently.

5. Benefits of carrying out Dry Run

Before the go-live weekend, a Dry Run (dress rehearsal) allows the team to test the full cutover plan in a controlled environment. Performing this from ECC Production to S/4HANA Production reveals any environment-specific issues in advance and provides confidence in your cutover timeline and activities. This proactive step greatly contributes to a smooth go-live.

6. Predefined Acceptance Criteria

Testing often reveals hundreds of issues. Without predefined acceptance criteria, it becomes difficult for project management to make informed go/no-go decisions. One recommended approach is the Severity Classification Method, which categorizes defects as follows:

  • Showstopper: The process cannot proceed
  • Workaround: The process can proceed with a workaround
  • No Impact: No effect on processes

By classifying all issues, the project team can focus on resolving showstoppers within the test timeline. Tools like SAP Cloud ALM offer efficient defect tracking and allow configuration of severity levels to streamline defect management.

Final Thoughts

Migrating to S/4HANA is a complex endeavor, but with thorough preparation, collaborative design, rigorous testing, and a proactive mindset, it is entirely achievable. I hope these lessons offer valuable guidance to others preparing for or undertaking their own migrations.

 

 

[1] SAP S/4HANA Simplification Item Check — How to do … — SAP Community

[2] Planning a Learning Needs Analysis

[3] Analyzing each phase of SAP Activate

[4] Conducting Design Workshops

[5] Analyzing each phase of SAP Activate

[6] Explaining the Test Strategy

entry.title: how to trigger staple functionality in local printer with output from sap public cloud system
entry.content:
Hi All,

We have requirement to trigger the staple functionality from sap public cloud system. I have checked in sap note and print queue settings but there is no option related to staple.

Please advise if its possible

entry.title: Is SAP Stock Room Management compatible with Handling Unit Management and Serial Number Management?
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

I am currently working on a project involving SAP Stock Room Management in S/4HANA. I would like to confirm whether Stock Room Management is fully compatible with Handling Unit Management (HUM),  Serial Number Management functionalities and Fiori applications. 

From the SAP documentation, it is not fully clear whether it's compatible or not.

Has anyone successfully implemented HUM and Serial Number Management with Stock Room Management?

Any official SAP reference or clarification would be highly appreciated!

Thank you in advance for your support!

entry.title: Persistence of CDS Views in HANA Database Local Table
entry.content:
Dear All, 

Is there an option to persist CDS view data in the HANA database when HANA DB is being used as a middleware or data warehouse?

In my scenario, I have legacy data stored in HANA DB and OLTP data in S/4HANA. I would like to create a combined view of both datasets and expose it to SAC. The goal is to improve performance by avoiding direct consumption of the CDS view in SAC.

In my case, a custom SQL view (ZSQL) has been created in S/4HANA using CDS. How can this view be accessed or persisted in a separate HANA database that is acting as a middleware or data warehouse?
I have two HANA databases: one serving OLAP purposes (data warehouse/middleware) and the other being the S/4HANA OLTP system.
